{{Quote|The Hapans will put the fear into those warships.|Commodore [[Turk Brand]]|<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]''</ref>}}
The '''Hapan Royal Navy''', also known as '''Hapan Fleets''', '''Hapan Royal Fleet''' or the '''Consortium Navy''', was the primary military forces of the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]], a matriarchal monarchy based in the [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]] of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].

==History==
{{Quote|True to our pirate roots, the Hapans prefer swift, ruthless strikes. Hurt an enemy at the start of an engagement and he is yours, for as the fight progresses, his fear of you will intensify and will become your ally.|Prince [[Isolder]] discussing Hapan battle tactics with [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]|<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse" />}}
The Hapan Royal Navy had its origins in the [[Lorell Raiders]], a band of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] who raided [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] shipping from the concealment of the [[Transitory Mists]] that isolated the Cluster. After the pirate fleet was [[Battle of Lorell|virtually annihilated]] by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], a matriarchal monarchy headed by a [[Queen Mother]] arose on [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]] and began to subdue the other planets within the Cluster. By approximately [[3100 BBY]], the newly-unified Hapes Consortium sealed its borders upon the command of the [[Isolationist Queen Mother|reigning Queen Mother]].<ref name="CPL">''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]''</ref> At around this time, the Navy's primary role became focused on defending the Consortium's borders against smugglers, pirates, and other intruders. It is also likely the Navy participated in subduing the rebellious [[Rifle Worlds]] when they attempted to secede from the Consortium sometime between [[3000 BBY]] and [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]].

[[File:BattleOfAndalia-EGW.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A Hapan fleet of [[Hapan Battle Dragon|Battle Dragons]] and [[Nova-class battle cruiser|''Nova''-class battle cruisers]] at the [[Battle of Andalia]].]]
While there are no records of major confrontations between the Hapan Navy and the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Starfleet]] during the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], there were a number of skirmishes. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], a small Imperial fleet under the command of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Osvald Teshik]] violated the Consortium's borders on the orders of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. The force arrived above Hapes proper before it proceeded to the [[Andalia system/Legends|Andalia system]], where it was [[Battle of Andalia|destroyed]] by the Hapan Royal Navy. An Imperial [[snoopship]] later succeeded in recovering Teshik.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> Other military encounters between the two powers resulted in the Hapan capture of several Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]].<ref name="CPL" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], an honor guard detachment from the Consortium Navy accompanied [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Isolder]] to [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] capital of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in the [[Chume'da]]'s bid for the hand of Princess [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]. Later that year, the Hapan Royal Navy fought against [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]] at the [[Battle of Dathomir (Hunt for Zsinj)|Battle of Dathomir]].<ref name="CPL" />

After the battle, the Consortium entered into an alliance with the New Republic. However, the [[Hapan Treaty]] made clear that the Hapan Fleet would remain independent from the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Defense Fleet]], as the vast majority of the Hapes Consortium preferred to remain a smaller cog in galactic affairs rather than risk being seen as a political rival to the New Republic.<ref name="CTD">''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]''</ref>

In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], Ambassador Solo approached the Consortium for assistance in the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|struggle against]] the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. After a rancorous debate, the Consortium committed the Hapan Royal Navy to the conflict. The Hapan fleet mustered at [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]] alongside a New Republic flotilla under the command of [[Commodore/Legends|Commodore]] [[Turk Brand]] in anticipation of a Yuuzhan Vong attack on nearby [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. However, [[Battle of Fondor|the attack]] came at [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]]. The joint task force soon arrived at the planet, where they quickly arrayed themselves for battle. However, three-fourths of the Hapan Navy was destroyed when [[Thrackan Sal-Solo]] fired [[Centerpoint Station]] at the Yuuzhan Vong fleet.<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse" /> Some survivors of the Fondor debacle deserted and subsequently became pirates.<ref name="DJ">''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Journey]]''</ref>

[[File:BattleofFondor.jpg|thumb|left|175px|The Hapan fleet is devastated at Fondor.]]
By [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]], secret [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] concealed within the Transitory Mists had rebuilt the Consortium Navy. Under the temporary command of [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Jagged Fel]], these forces [[Battle of Hapes (Yuuzhan Vong War)|repelled]] a Yuuzhan Vong invasion force.<ref name="DJ" /> In [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]], a Hapan Royal Navy task force under the command of Queen Mother [[Tenel Ka Djo]] helped annihilate a Yuuzhan Vong fleet at the [[Second Battle of Obroa-skai]].<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way">''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]''</ref> The Hapan Navy also participated in the [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar|Recapture of Coruscant]].<ref name="The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref>

During the [[Dark Nest Crisis]], a Consortium task force under [[Dukat]] [[Aleson Gray]] supported [[The Colony]] at the [[Battle of Qoribu]].<ref name="Dark Nest I: The Joiner King">''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]''</ref>

In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], Corellia attempted to draw the Consortium into its camp with an assassination attempt against Queen Mother Tenel Ka and [[Allana Djo Solo|her heir]]. The plan was to have a [[Heritage Council|pro-Corellian faction]] take the Queen Mother's place. Three [[Corellian Dreadnaught]]s joined the rebel Hapan fleet, but support from the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Fleet]] helped the loyalist Navy [[Battle of Hapes (Second Galactic Civil War)|stop the coup]].<ref name="Legacy of the Force: Tempest">''[[Legacy of the Force: Tempest]]''</ref>

During the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], the Consortium Navy provided critical support to the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]]. The [[Hapan Home Fleet]] fought under [[Galactic Alliance Guard]] Colonel and aspirant [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] at the [[Battle of Kuat (Second Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Kuat]] against the rebellious [[Confederation]].<ref name="Legacy of the Force: Inferno">''[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno]]''</ref> However, when Solo began to raze [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] in order to lure enemy forces away from [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]], Queen Mother Tenel Ka withdraw her fleets and supported the [[Jedi Coalition]]. The Hapan Navy fought against Solo at the [[Battle of Kashyyyk (Second Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Kashyyyk]] and the [[Battle of Uroro Station]].<ref name="Legacy of the Force: Invincible">''[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible]]''</ref>

The Hapes Consortium was neutral during the [[Sith–Imperial War]] and the [[Second Imperial Civil War]].<ref name="Legacy 2006 9">[[Legacy (2006) 9|''Legacy'' (2006) 9]]</ref>

==Organization==
[[File:BattleDragonsAtCoruscant-TEC.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Hapan Navy honor guard assembled at [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]].]]
Circa 8 ABY, [[Isolder]] described the forces of [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]] as "billions of warriors, thousands of starships."<ref name="CPL" />

During the build up prior to the [[Battle of Dathomir (Hunt for Zsinj)|Battle of Dathomir]], [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Isolder]] called a group of "about eighty destroyers" a Hapan fleet.<ref name="CPL" /> While vague, these comments give some insight to their Order of Battle comparable to other space navies.

The Hapan Royal Navy was centered around a fleet of hundreds of [[Hapan Battle Dragon]]s, with an honor fleet containing sixty-three, one representing each member [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the Consortium. They were backed up by [[Nova-class battle cruiser|''Nova''-class battle cruisers]] and [[Beta Cruiser]]s, as well as X-Wings, [[Miy'til starfighter]]s, and [[Miy'til assault bomber]]s. [[Hetrinar assault bomber]]s were also sometimes deployed by Nova-class cruisers for defensive purposes.

==Tactics and Technology==
To counteract the slow recharge rate of [[Hapan turbolaser]]s, Naval officers bunched warships together to create walls of fire to cripple enemy ships in swift assaults. If a unit did not destroy all enemies in the first pass, it ran the risk of taking heavy fire while its weapons recycled.

Despite the Hapan's growing relationship with the New Republic from [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] forward,<ref name="CTD" /> they did not negotiate an exchange of turbolaser technology until [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], when it was used as an incentive to bring the Hapan military into the Yuuzhan Vong War. After the exchange in technology Hapes technology particularly in military was estimated to be as much as seven hundred years behind the galaxy.
